title: Different Provinces, Same Industrial Policies
text:
Different Provinces, Same Industrial Policies

Tilly Zhang

Gavekal Dragonomics | 8 Jul 2026

China’s leadership is increasingly concerned about waste and duplication in the industrial policies pursued by local governments, and wants to prevent them from all crowding into the same popular sectors. Top leader Xi Jinping put it bluntly in remarks at the Central Economic Work Conference in December: “Some localities don’t pay attention to reality and blindly chase trends. They see people going into chips, and they do the same… Why should everyone all choose to do the same job?” The central government has repeatedly admonished local authorities to pursue an industrial policy “based on local conditions,” meaning one adapted to their particular competitive advantages rather than copied from somewhere else.In practice, though, it is hard to change the incentives that lead local governments to herd into fashionable sectors. The latest provincial five-year plans show that local authorities are following central guidance to shift their attention to a newer set of industries that are consistent with the national five-year plan. But there is not much evidence that the provinces are adapting those central priorities to their local conditions: the provincial plans are still extremely similar. Because the central government continues to tell local governments which industries to favor, local governments continue to focus on a limited set of politically encouraged industries.
